Pterophylla vescoi, also known as Chinese ladder fern, is a plant species that is native to China and Japan, and is characterized by its unique, ladder-like fronds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the ability to stimulate blood flow, reduce inflammation, and improve liver function. The bioactive constituents of Pterophylla vescoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or made into capsules, and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
